_doc: Maps wiki page slugs to map features. Stand-in for per-page frontmatter; same shape works either way. 'features' = list of {layer, id?, match?}. 'focus' = optional override for fly-to. 'layers_on' = preferred default layer set when this page is the focus.

claim-transmission-immediate-blocker.narrative: Generation has outpaced wires. The 400 kV east–west backbone is incomplete; western evacuation is constrained by legacy 132 kV; cross-border capacity lags behind installed MW.

claim-storage-physical-fix.narrative: Run-of-river cannot reschedule the monsoon. Storage is the clearest physical answer — the shortlist below carries dry-season energy that the present fleet cannot.

claim-sediment-core-issue.narrative: Sediment is a first-order design constraint, not a footnote. Koshi alone moves ~120 Mt/year; Kali Gandaki turbines erode within years.

claim-stranded-generation.narrative: Monsoon 2025 spilled 700–800 MW daily — energy that exists but cannot be moved or sold.

master-thesis.title: Master Thesis: Nepal is dispatch-poor, not resource-poor

steps.subtitle: 1. The hydrology delivers a monsoon pulse, not firm power.
2. The fleet is >90% run-of-river. It mirrors the hydrograph by design.
3. There is almost no storage to reshape the year. 5 m³/capita vs 900 global.
4. And the wires lag behind the megawatts. Spill is now a transmission story.
5. Storage is the clearest physical answer. The shortlist already exists.
